archimate:ApplicationComponent.name: Service Registry
archimate:ApplicationComponent.documentation:
Definition: Service Registry ABB is an Application Component that enables the registering of system services within a catalogue to facilitate the discovery of services.
Source: ISA2 - EIA Action
Source reference: http://data.europa.eu/eli/dec/2015/2240/oj
Example: The following implementation is an example on how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): jUDDI - jUDDI is an open source Java implementation of OASIS Universal Description, Discovery, and Integration (UDDI) specification for (Web) Services. https://juddi.apache.org/
Interoperability Dimension: Structural IoP
LOST view: Technical view - application
Interoperability Saliency: The Service Registry Component ABB is a key interoperability enabler because it supports to achieve technical interoperability by provisioning and consuming back-office services as stated in the EIF recommendation n°44: "Put in place catalogues of public services, public data, and interoperability solutions and use common models for describing them."
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/ServiceRegistryApplicationComponent
ABB name: eira:ServiceRegistryApplicationComponent
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/ServiceRegistryApplicationComponent
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication

properties.value: Service Registry ABB is an Application Component that enables the registering of system services within a catalogue to facilitate the discovery of services.

The following implementation is an example on how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): jUDDI - jUDDI is an open source Java implementation of OASIS Universal Description, Discovery, and Integration (UDDI) specification for (Web) Services. https://juddi.apache.org/

The Service Registry Component ABB is a key interoperability enabler because it supports to achieve technical interoperability by provisioning and consuming back-office services as stated in the EIF recommendation n°44: "Put in place catalogues of public services, public data, and interoperability solutions and use common models for describing them."
